attacking_style: Bridges to breakaways on penultimate climbs rather than launching solo attacks, then relies on superior finishing speed in technical finales. Prefers tactical positioning over pure power displays.
team_tactics: Limited team visibility in observations; appears to operate independently once positioned, suggesting trust in personal finishing ability over heavy domestique support in critical moments.
terrain_preference: Technical, wet finales with short punchy climbs before finish. Excels when race requires combination of climbing to bridge gaps and sprint speed to finish. Sustained climbs for GC defense also favorable.
vulnerability: Observations show defensive GC riding rather than aggressive stage hunting when protecting lead, suggesting potential vulnerability to sustained attacks that force reactive racing rather than tactical positioning.
key_patterns: Bridges gaps solo to select groups rather than staying with peloton (2 instances)
Unexpected finishing speed against pure sprinters in reduced bunch scenarios (2 instances)
Conservative energy management when defending GC leads rather than seeking stage wins (2 instances)
Performs well in adverse weather conditions, specifically wet technical courses (2 instances)
form_trajectory: positive: Won stage then successfully defended GC lead with tactical maturity, showing both offensive firepower and defensive race management
